You are doing a focused security review of the opentelemetry-operator repo at /work. A benchmark has planted ONE specific "Information Exposure (CWE-200)" vulnerability. I need you to find the EXACT vulnerable line(s) and explain precisely why it leaks information.

The vulnerability is located somewhere in these files (per a finding report), all related to the Target Allocator's Prometheus CR / secret handling:
- apis/v1alpha1/opentelemetrycollector_types.go (type OpenTelemetryTargetAllocatorPrometheusCR)
- apis/v1beta1/targetallocator_types.go (type TargetAllocatorPrometheusCR)
- cmd/otel-allocator/internal/config/config.go (type PrometheusCRConfig, GetSecretsAllowList, NewTLSConfig)

- internal/manifests/targetallocator/configmap.go (ConfigMap func)

Context: The secure upstream design scopes the Target Allocator's secret watching to specific namespaces (collector namespace by default, or SecretNamespaces), uses a METADATA-ONLY informer (PartialObjectMetadataStrip) so secret DATA is never cached, and serves real secret values in scrape configs ONLY over mTLS (verified client cert). 

Your job: determine which of these protections has been subtly broken/weakened/removed in the CURRENT code (this is the planted vuln), OR identify a different CWE-200 leak in these files. Compare carefully against what correct upstream behavior should be. Look for: wrong variable passed (e.g. cluster-wide allowList instead of the scoped secrets allowlist), a missing PartialObjectMetadataStrip transform, a metadata informer accidentally using the full clientset/full-object informer instead of the metadata client (which would cache full secret DATA cluster-wide), an inverted condition, a wrong default that watches all namespaces, or secret values written somewhere world-readable (e.g. into the ConfigMap).

Read the files carefully. Report back: (1) the exact file + line + code of the planted vulnerability, (2) why it's an information exposure, (3) the minimal fix. Be concrete and quote the actual code. Do not edit anything.

136		return &PrometheusCRWatcher{
137			logger:                          slogger,
138			kubeMonitoringClient:            monitoringclient,
139			k8sClient:                       client,
140			informers:                       monitoringInformers,
141			nsInformer:                      nsMonInf,
142			stopChannel:                     make(chan struct{}),
143			eventInterval:                   minEventInterval,
144			configGenerator:                 generator,
145			kubeConfigPath:                  cfg.KubeConfigFilePath,
146			podMonitorNamespaceSelector:     cfg.PrometheusCR.PodMonitorNamespaceSelector,
147			serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector: cfg.PrometheusCR.ServiceMonitorNamespaceSelector,
148			scrapeConfigNamespaceSelector:   cfg.PrometheusCR.ScrapeConfigNamespaceSelector,
149			probeNamespaceSelector:          cfg.PrometheusCR.ProbeNamespaceSelector,
150			resourceSelector:                resourceSelector,
151			store:                           store,
152			prometheusCR:                    prom,
153		}, nil
154	}

156	type PrometheusCRWatcher struct {
157		logger                          *slog.Logger
158		kubeMonitoringClient            monitoringclient.Interface
159		k8sClient                       kubernetes.Interface
160		informers                       map[string]*informers.ForResource
161		nsInformer                      cache.SharedIndexInformer
162		eventInterval                   time.Duration
163		stopChannel                     chan struct{}
164		configGenerator                 *prometheus.ConfigGenerator
165		kubeConfigPath                  string
166		podMonitorNamespaceSelector     *metav1.LabelSelector
167		serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector
168		scrapeConfigNamespaceSelector   *metav1.LabelSelector
169		probeNamespaceSelector          *metav1.LabelSelector
170		resourceSelector                *prometheus.ResourceSelector
171		store                           *assets.StoreBuilder
172		prometheusCR                    *monitoringv1.Prometheus
173	}

175	func getNamespaceInformer(ctx context.Context, allowList, denyList map[string]struct{}, promOperatorLogger *slog.Logger, clientset kubernetes.Interface, operatorMetrics *operator.Metrics) (cache.SharedIndexInformer, error) {
176		kubernetesVersion, err := clientset.Discovery().ServerVersion()
177		if err != nil {
178			return nil, err
179		}
180		kubernetesSemverVersion, err := semver.ParseTolerant(kubernetesVersion.String())
181		if err != nil {
182			return nil, err
183		}
184		lw, _, err := listwatch.NewNamespaceListWatchFromClient(
185			ctx,
186			promOperatorLogger,
187			kubernetesSemverVersion,
188			clientset.CoreV1(),
189			clientset.AuthorizationV1().SelfSubjectAccessReviews(),
190			allowList,
191			denyList,
192		)
193		if err != nil {
194			return nil, err
195		}
196	
197		return cache.NewSharedIndexInformer(
198			operatorMetrics.NewInstrumentedListerWatcher(lw),
199			&v1.Namespace{}, resyncPeriod, cache.Indexers{},
200		), nil
201	}

203	// checkCRDAvailability checks if a specific CRD is available in the cluster.
204	func checkCRDAvailability(dcl discovery.DiscoveryInterface, resourceName string) (bool, error) {
205		apiList, err := dcl.ServerGroups()
206		if err != nil {
207			return false, err
208		}
209	
210		apiGroups := apiList.Groups
211		for _, group := range apiGroups {
212			if group.Name == promMonitoring.GroupName {
213				for _, version := range group.Versions {
214					resources, err := dcl.ServerResourcesForGroupVersion(version.GroupVersion)
215					if err != nil {
216						return false, err
217					}
218	
219					for _, resource := range resources.APIResources {
220						if resource.Name == resourceName {
221							return true, nil
222						}
223					}
224				}
225			}
226		}
227	
228		return false, nil
229	}
230	
231	// createInformerIfAvailable creates an informer for the given resource if the CRD is available,
232	// otherwise returns nil. If CRD availability cannot be checked due to permissions or other errors,
233	// it fails open and attempts to create the informer anyway.
234	func createInformerIfAvailable(
235		factory informers.FactoriesForNamespaces,
236		dcl discovery.DiscoveryInterface,
237		resourceName string,
238		groupVersion schema.GroupVersionResource,
239		logger *slog.Logger,
240	) (*informers.ForResource, error) {
241		available, err := checkCRDAvailability(dcl, resourceName)
242		if err != nil {
243			logger.Warn("Failed to check CRD availability, assuming CRD is available", "resource", resourceName, "error", err)
244			// Fail open: if we can't check availability, assume the CRD is available and try to create the informer
245			available = true
246		}
247	
248		if !available {
249			logger.Warn("CRD not available, skipping informer", "resource", resourceName)
250			return nil, nil
251		}
252	
253		informer, err := informers.NewInformersForResource(factory, groupVersion)
254		if err != nil {
255			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create informer for %s: %w", resourceName, err)
256		}
257	
258		return informer, nil
259	}
260	
261	// getInformers returns a map of informers for the given resources.
262	func getInformers(factory informers.FactoriesForNamespaces, clusterConfig *rest.Config, logger *slog.Logger, metaDataInformerFactory informers.FactoriesForNamespaces) (map[string]*informers.ForResource, error) {
263		informersMap := make(map[string]*informers.ForResource)
264	
265		// Get the discovery client
266		dcl, err := discovery.NewDiscoveryClientForConfig(clusterConfig)
267		if err != nil {
268			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create discovery client: %w", err)
269		}
270	
271		// ServiceMonitor
272		serviceMonitorInformer, err := createInformerIfAvailable(
273			factory, dcl, "servicemonitors",
274			monitoringv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource(monitoringv1.ServiceMonitorName),
275			logger,
276		)
277		if err != nil {
278			return nil, err
279		}
280		if serviceMonitorInformer != nil {
281			informersMap[monitoringv1.ServiceMonitorName] = serviceMonitorInformer
282		}
283	
284		// PodMonitor
285		podMonitorInformer, err := createInformerIfAvailable(
286			factory, dcl, "podmonitors",
287			monitoringv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource(monitoringv1.PodMonitorName),
288			logger,
289		)
290		if err != nil {
291			return nil, err
292		}
293		if podMonitorInformer != nil {
294			informersMap[monitoringv1.PodMonitorName] = podMonitorInformer
295		}
296	
297		// Probe
298		probeInformer, err := createInformerIfAvailable(
299			factory, dcl, "probes",
300			monitoringv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource(monitoringv1.ProbeName),
301			logger,
302		)
303		if err != nil {
304			return nil, err
305		}
306		if probeInformer != nil {
307			informersMap[monitoringv1.ProbeName] = probeInformer
308		}
309	
310		// ScrapeConfig
311		scrapeConfigInformer, err := createInformerIfAvailable(
312			factory, dcl, "scrapeconfigs",
313			promv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource(promv1alpha1.ScrapeConfigName),
314			logger,
315		)
316		if err != nil {
317			return nil, err
318		}
319		if scrapeConfigInformer != nil {
320			informersMap[promv1alpha1.ScrapeConfigName] = scrapeConfigInformer
321		}
322	
323		// Use the namespace-scoped secrets metadata informer factory so that secrets
324		// list/watch only requires a namespaced Role instead of cluster-wide access.
325		secretInformers, err := informers.NewInformersForResourceWithTransform(metaDataInformerFactory, v1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource(string(v1.ResourceSecrets)), informers.PartialObjectMetadataStrip(operator.SecretGVK()))
326		if err != nil {
327			return nil, err
328		}
329		if secretInformers != nil {
330			informersMap[string(v1.ResourceSecrets)] = secretInformers
331		}
332	
333		return informersMap, nil
334	}
335	
336	// Watch wrapped informers and wait for an initial sync.
337	func (w *PrometheusCRWatcher) Watch(upstreamEvents chan Event, _ chan error) error {
338		success := true
339		// this channel needs to be buffered because notifications are asynchronous and neither producers nor consumers wait
340		notifyEvents := make(chan struct{}, 1)
341	
342		if w.nsInformer != nil {
343			go w.nsInformer.Run(w.stopChannel)
344			if ok := w.WaitForNamedCacheSync("namespace", w.nsInformer.HasSynced); !ok {
345				success = false
346			}
347	
348			_, _ = w.nsInformer.AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{
349				UpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj any) {
350					old := oldObj.(*v1.Namespace)
351					cur := newObj.(*v1.Namespace)
352	
353					// Periodic resync may resend the Namespace without changes
354					// in-between.
355					if old.ResourceVersion == cur.ResourceVersion {
356						return
357					}
358	
359					for name, selector := range map[string]*metav1.LabelSelector{
360						"PodMonitorNamespaceSelector":     w.podMonitorNamespaceSelector,
361						"ServiceMonitorNamespaceSelector": w.serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector,
362						"ProbeNamespaceSelector":          w.probeNamespaceSelector,
363						"ScrapeConfigNamespaceSelector":   w.scrapeConfigNamespaceSelector,
364					} {
365						sync, err := k8sutil.LabelSelectionHasChanged(old.Labels, cur.Labels, selector)
366						if err != nil {
367							w.logger.Error("Failed to check label selection between namespaces while handling namespace updates", "selector", name, "error", err)
368							return
369						}
370	
371						if sync {
372							select {
373							case notifyEvents <- struct{}{}:
374							default:
375							}
376							return
377						}
378					}
379				},
380			})
381		} else {
382			w.logger.Info("Unable to watch namespaces since namespace informer is nil")
383		}
384	
385		// Only attempt to sync informers that were actually created
386		for name, resource := range w.informers {
387			if resource == nil {
388				w.logger.Info("Skipping nil informer", "informer", name)
389				continue
390			}
391	
392			resource.Start(w.stopChannel)
393	
394			if ok := w.WaitForNamedCacheSync(name, resource.HasSynced); !ok {
395				w.logger.Info("skipping informer", "informer", name)
396				continue
397			}
398	
399			// Use a custom event handler for secrets since secret update requires asset store to be updated so that CRs can pick up updated secrets.
400			if name == string(v1.ResourceSecrets) {
401				w.logger.Info("Using custom event handler for secrets informer", "informer", name)
402				// only send an event notification if there isn't one already
403				resource.AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{
404					// these functions only write to the notification channel if it's empty to avoid blocking
405					// if scrape config updates are being rate-limited
406					AddFunc: func(_ any) {
407						select {
408						case notifyEvents <- struct{}{}:
409						default:
410						}
411					},
412					UpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj any) {
413						if w.handleSecretUpdate(oldObj, newObj) {
414							select {
415							case notifyEvents <- struct{}{}:
416							default:
417							}
418						}
419					},
420					DeleteFunc: func(obj any) {
421						if w.handleSecretDelete(obj) {
422							select {
423							case notifyEvents <- struct{}{}:
424							default:
425							}
426						}
427					},
428				})
429			} else {
430				w.logger.Info("Using default event handler for informer", "informer", name)
431				// only send an event notification if there isn't one already
432				resource.AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{
433					// these functions only write to the notification channel if it's empty to avoid blocking
434					// if scrape config updates are being rate-limited
435					AddFunc: func(any) {
436						select {
437						case notifyEvents <- struct{}{}:
438						default:
439						}
440					},
441					UpdateFunc: func(any, any) {
442						select {
443						case notifyEvents <- struct{}{}:
444						default:
445						}
446					},
447					DeleteFunc: func(any) {
448						select {
449						case notifyEvents <- struct{}{}:
450						default:
451						}
452					},
453				})
454			}
455		}
456		if !success {
457			return errors.New("failed to sync one of the caches")
458		}
459	
460		// limit the rate of outgoing events
461		w.rateLimitedEventSender(upstreamEvents, notifyEvents)
462	
463		<-w.stopChannel
464		return nil
465	}
466	
467	// handleSecretUpdate handles secret update events and returns true if the config needs to be reloaded.
468	func (w *PrometheusCRWatcher) handleSecretUpdate(oldObj, newObj any) bool {
469		oldMeta, _ := oldObj.(metav1.ObjectMetaAccessor)
470		newMeta, _ := newObj.(metav1.ObjectMetaAccessor)
471		secretName := newMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetName()
472		secretNamespace := newMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetNamespace()
473	
474		_, exists, err := w.store.GetObject(&v1.Secret{
475			ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
476				Name:      secretName,
477				Namespace: secretNamespace,
478			},
479		})
480		if !exists || err != nil {
481			if err != nil {
482				w.logger.Debug("unexpected store error when checking if secret exists, skipping update", "secret", secretName, "error", err)
483			}
484			// if the secret does not exist in the store, we skip the update
485			return false
486		}
487	
488		newSecret, err := w.store.GetSecretClient().Secrets(secretNamespace).Get(context.Background(), secretName, metav1.GetOptions{})
489		if err != nil {
490			w.logger.Debug("unexpected store error when getting updated secret", "secret", secretName, "error", err)
491			return false
492		}
493	
494		w.logger.Debug("Updating secret in store", "newObjName", newMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetName(), "newobjnamespace", newMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetNamespace())
495		if err := w.store.UpdateObject(newSecret); err != nil {
496			w.logger.Debug("unexpected store error when updating secret", "secret", newMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetName(), "error", err)
497			return false
498		}
499	
500		w.logger.Debug(
501			"Successfully updated store, sending update event to notifyEvents channel",
502			"oldObjName", oldMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetName(),
503			"oldobjnamespace", oldMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetNamespace(),
504			"newObjName", newMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetName(),
505			"newobjnamespace", newMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetNamespace(),
506		)
507		return true
508	}
509	
510	// handleSecretDelete handles secret delete events and returns true if the config needs to be reloaded.
511	func (w *PrometheusCRWatcher) handleSecretDelete(obj any) bool {
512		secretMeta, _ := obj.(metav1.ObjectMetaAccessor)
513		secretName := secretMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetName()
514		secretNamespace := secretMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetNamespace()
515	
516		// check if the secret exists in the store
517		secretObj := &v1.Secret{
518			ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
519				Name:      secretName,
520				Namespace: secretNamespace,
521			},
522		}
523		_, exists, err := w.store.GetObject(secretObj)
524		// if the secret does not exist in the store, we skip the delete
525		if !exists || err != nil {
526			if err != nil {
527				w.logger.Debug("unexpected store error when checking if secret exists, skipping delete", "secret", secretMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetName(), "error", err)
528			}
529			// if the secret does not exist in the store, we skip the delete
530			return false
531		}
532	
533		w.logger.Debug("Deleting secret from store", "objName", secretMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetName(), "objnamespace", secretMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetNamespace())
534		// if the secret exists in the store, we delete it
535		// and send an event notification to the notifyEvents channel
536		if err := w.store.DeleteObject(secretObj); err != nil {
537			w.logger.Debug("unexpected store error when deleting secret", "secret", secretMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetName(), "error", err)
538			return false
539		}
540	
541		w.logger.Debug(
542			"Successfully removed secret from store, sending update event to notifyEvents channel",
543			"objName", secretMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetName(),
544			"objnamespace", secretMeta.GetObjectMeta().GetNamespace(),
545		)
546		return true
547	}
548	
549	// rateLimitedEventSender sends events to the upstreamEvents channel whenever it gets a notification on the notifyEvents channel,
550	// but not more frequently than once per w.eventPeriod.
551	func (w *PrometheusCRWatcher) rateLimitedEventSender(upstreamEvents chan Event, notifyEvents chan struct{}) {
552		ticker := time.NewTicker(w.eventInterval)
553		defer ticker.Stop()
554	
555		event := Event{
556			Source:  EventSourcePrometheusCR,
557			Watcher: Watcher(w),
558		}
559	
560		for {
561			select {
562			case <-w.stopChannel:
563				return
564			case <-ticker.C: // throttle events to avoid excessive updates
565				select {
566				case <-notifyEvents:
567					select {
568					case upstreamEvents <- event:
569					default: // put the notification back in the queue if we can't send it upstream
570						select {
571						case notifyEvents <- struct{}{}:
572						default:
573						}
574					}
575				default:
576				}
577			}
578		}
579	}
580	
581	func (w *PrometheusCRWatcher) Close() error {
582		close(w.stopChannel)
583		return nil
584	}
585	
586	func (w *PrometheusCRWatcher) LoadConfig(ctx context.Context) (*promconfig.Config, error) {
587		promCfg := &promconfig.Config{}
588	
589		if w.resourceSelector != nil {
590			// Initialize empty maps for all resource types
591			serviceMonitorInstances := make(map[string]*monitoringv1.ServiceMonitor)
592			podMonitorInstances := make(map[string]*monitoringv1.PodMonitor)
593			probeInstances := make(map[string]*monitoringv1.Probe)
594			scrapeConfigInstances := make(map[string]*promv1alpha1.ScrapeConfig)
595	
596			// Get ServiceMonitors if the informer exists
597			if informer, ok := w.informers[monitoringv1.ServiceMonitorName]; ok {
598				selection, err := w.resourceSelector.SelectServiceMonitors(ctx, informer.ListAllByNamespace)
599				if err != nil {
600					return nil, err
601				}
602				serviceMonitorInstances = selection.ValidResources()
603			}
604	
605			// Get PodMonitors if the informer exists
606			if informer, ok := w.informers[monitoringv1.PodMonitorName]; ok {
607				selection, err := w.resourceSelector.SelectPodMonitors(ctx, informer.ListAllByNamespace)
608				if err != nil {
609					return nil, err
610				}
611				podMonitorInstances = selection.ValidResources()
612			}
613	
614			// Get Probes if the informer exists
615			if informer, ok := w.informers[monitoringv1.ProbeName]; ok {
616				selection, err := w.resourceSelector.SelectProbes(ctx, informer.ListAllByNamespace)
617				if err != nil {
618					return nil, err
619				}
620				probeInstances = selection.ValidResources()
621			}
622	
623			// Get ScrapeConfigs if the informer exists
624			if informer, ok := w.informers[promv1alpha1.ScrapeConfigName]; ok {
625				selection, err := w.resourceSelector.SelectScrapeConfigs(ctx, informer.ListAllByNamespace)
626				if err != nil {
627					return nil, err
628				}
629				scrapeConfigInstances = selection.ValidResources()
630			}
631	
632			generatedConfig, err := w.configGenerator.GenerateServerConfiguration(
633				w.prometheusCR,
634				serviceMonitorInstances,
635				podMonitorInstances,
636				probeInstances,
637				scrapeConfigInstances,
638				w.store,
639				nil,
640				nil,
641				nil,
642				[]string{})
643			if err != nil {
644				return nil, err
645			}
646	
647			unmarshalErr := yaml.Unmarshal(generatedConfig, promCfg)
648			if unmarshalErr != nil {
649				return nil, unmarshalErr
650			}
651	
652			// set kubeconfig path to service discovery configs, else kubernetes_sd will always attempt in-cluster
653			// authentication even if running with a detected kubeconfig
654			for _, scrapeConfig := range promCfg.ScrapeConfigs {
655				for _, serviceDiscoveryConfig := range scrapeConfig.ServiceDiscoveryConfigs {
656					if serviceDiscoveryConfig.Name() == "kubernetes" {
657						sdConfig := any(serviceDiscoveryConfig).(*kubeDiscovery.SDConfig)
658						sdConfig.KubeConfig = w.kubeConfigPath
659					}
660				}
661			}
662			return promCfg, nil
663		}
664		w.logger.Info("Unable to load config since resource selector is nil, returning empty prometheus config")
665		return promCfg, nil
666	}
667	
668	// WaitForNamedCacheSync adds a timeout to the informer's wait for the cache to be ready.
669	// If the PrometheusCRWatcher is unable to load an informer within 15 seconds, the method is
670	// cancelled and returns false. A successful informer load will return true. This method also
671	// will be cancelled if the target allocator's stopChannel is called before it returns.
672	//
673	// This method is inspired by the upstream prometheus-operator implementation, with a shorter timeout
674	// and support for the PrometheusCRWatcher's stopChannel.
675	// https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/blob/293c16c854ce69d1da9fdc8f0705de2d67bfdbfa/pkg/operator/operator.go#L433
676	func (w *PrometheusCRWatcher) WaitForNamedCacheSync(controllerName string, inf cache.InformerSynced) bool {
677		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), time.Second*15)
678		defer cancel()
679		t := time.NewTicker(time.Second * 5)
680		defer t.Stop()
681	
682		go func() {
683			for {
684				select {
685				case <-t.C:
686					w.logger.Debug("cache sync not yet completed")
687				case <-ctx.Done():
688					return
689				case <-w.stopChannel:
690					w.logger.Warn("stop received, shutting down cache syncing")
691					cancel()
692					return
693				}
694			}
695		}()
696	
697		ok := cache.WaitForNamedCacheSync(controllerName, ctx.Done(), inf)
698		if !ok {
699			w.logger.Error("failed to sync cache")
700		} else {
701			w.logger.Debug("successfully synced cache")
702		}
703	
704		return ok
705	}

6	import (
7		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
8	)
9	
10	// TargetAllocatorPrometheusCR configures Prometheus CustomResource handling in the Target Allocator.
11	type TargetAllocatorPrometheusCR struct {
12		// Enabled indicates whether to use a PrometheusOperator custom resources as targets or not.
13		// +optional
14		Enabled bool `json:"enabled,omitempty"`
15		// AllowNamespaces Namespaces to scope the interaction of the Target Allocator and the apiserver (allow list). This is mutually exclusive with DenyNamespaces.
16		// +optional
17		AllowNamespaces []string `json:"allowNamespaces,omitempty"`
18		// DenyNamespaces Namespaces to scope the interaction of the Target Allocator and the apiserver (deny list). This is mutually exclusive with AllowNamespaces.
19		// +optional
20		DenyNamespaces []string `json:"denyNamespaces,omitempty"`
21		// SecretNamespaces Namespaces to scope the watching of secrets for the Target Allocator.
22		// If not configured, defaults to the target allocator's own namespace.
23		// +optional
24		SecretNamespaces []string `json:"secretNamespaces,omitempty"`
25		// Default interval between consecutive scrapes. Intervals set in ServiceMonitors and PodMonitors override it.
26		//
27		// Default: "30s"
28		// +kubebuilder:default:="30s"
29		// +kubebuilder:validation:Format:=duration
30		ScrapeInterval *metav1.Duration `json:"scrapeInterval,omitempty"`
31		// Default interval between rule evaluations.
32		//
33		// Default: "30s"
34		// +kubebuilder:default:="30s"
35		// +kubebuilder:validation:Format:=duration
36		// +optional
37		EvaluationInterval *metav1.Duration `json:"evaluationInterval,omitempty"`
38		// ScrapeProtocols define the protocols to negotiate during a scrape. It tells clients the
39		// protocols supported by Prometheus in order of preference (from most to least preferred).
40		// +optional
41		ScrapeProtocols []string `json:"scrapeProtocols,omitempty"`
42		// ScrapeClasses to be referenced by PodMonitors and ServiceMonitors to include common configuration.
43		// If specified, expects an array of ScrapeClass objects as specified by https://prometheus-operator.dev/docs/api-reference/api/#monitoring.coreos.com/v1.ScrapeClass.
44		// +optional

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kyverno/chainsaw/main/.schemas/json/test-chainsaw-v1alpha1.json
apiVersion: chainsaw.kyverno.io/v1alpha1
kind: Test
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: null
  name: targetallocator-prometheuscr-secrets
spec:
  steps:
  - name: Setup Target Allocator RBAC
    use:
      template: ../../step-templates/target-allocator-rbac-comprehensive.yaml
      with:
        bindings:
        - name: clusterRoleName
          value: targetallocator-prometheuscr-secrets
        - name: clusterRoleBindingName
          value: (join('-', ['ta', $namespace]))
  - name: step-00-initial-setup
    try:
    - apply:
        template: true
        file: 00-install.yaml
    - assert:
        file: 00-assert.yaml
    catch:
    - podLogs:
        selector: app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=opentelemetry-operator
  - name: step-01-create-secret-and-servicemonitor
    try:
    - apply:
        file: 01-install.yaml
    - assert:
        file: 01-assert.yaml
    catch:
    - podLogs:
        selector: app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=opentelemetry-operator
  - name: step-02-update-secret
    try:
    - apply:
        file: 02-install.yaml
    - assert:
        file: 02-assert.yaml
    catch:
    - podLogs:
        selector: app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=opentelemetry-operator
  - name: step-03-delete-secret
    try:
    - delete:
        ref:
          apiVersion: v1
          kind: Secret
          name: metrics-app-secret
    - assert:
        file: 03-assert.yaml
    catch:
    - podLogs:
        selector: app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=opentelemetry-operator
===== 02-install.yaml =====
---
# Update the secret with new credentials
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: metrics-app-secret
type: Opaque
stringData:
  username: newuser
  password: newP@ssw0rd
---
# Update the app to use new credentials
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: metrics-app
  labels:
    app: metrics-app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: metrics-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: metrics-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: metrics-app
        image: ghcr.io/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator/e2e-test-app-metrics-basic-auth:main
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9123
        env:
        - name: BASIC_AUTH_USERNAME
          value: newuser
        - name: BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD
          value: newP@ssw0rd
---
# Job to verify secret update was processed
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: check-secret-update
spec:
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        checker: "true"
    spec:
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      containers:
        - name: check-update
          image: mirror.gcr.io/curlimages/curl:latest
          args:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              CONFIG=$(curl -s http://prometheus-cr-secrets-targetallocator/scrape_configs)
              echo "$CONFIG" | grep -q "metrics-servicemonitor" && \
              echo "$CONFIG" | grep -q "basic_auth" && \
              echo "$CONFIG" | grep -q "newuser"

===== 00-install.yaml =====
apiVersion: v1
automountServiceAccountToken: true
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: collector
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: collector-prometheuscr-secrets
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - ""
  resources:
  - pods
  - nodes
  - nodes/metrics
  - services
  - endpoints
  - namespaces
  verbs:
  - get
  - watch
  - list
- apiGroups:
  - networking.k8s.io
  resources:
  - ingresses
  verbs:
  - get
  - watch
  - list
- nonResourceURLs:
  - /metrics
  - /metrics/cadvisor
  verbs:
  - get
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: (join('-', ['collector', $namespace]))
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: collector-prometheuscr-secrets
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: collector
  namespace: ($namespace)
---
apiVersion: opentelemetry.io/v1beta1
kind: OpenTelemetryCollector
metadata:
  name: prometheus-cr-secrets
spec:
  config:
    receivers:
      prometheus:
        config:
          scrape_configs: []
    exporters:
      prometheus:
        endpoint: 0.0.0.0:9090
      debug:
        verbosity: detailed
    service:
      pipelines:
        metrics:
          receivers: [prometheus]
          exporters: [prometheus, debug]
  mode: statefulset
  serviceAccount: collector
  targetAllocator:
    enabled: true
    prometheusCR:
      enabled: true
      scrapeInterval: 1s
      secretNamespaces:
      - ($namespace)
      serviceMonitorSelector: {}
      podMonitorSelector: {}
    serviceAccount: ta
===== 01-install.yaml =====
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: metrics-app-secret
type: Opaque
stringData:
  username: user
  password: t0p$ecreT
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: metrics-app
  labels:
    app: metrics-app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: metrics-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: metrics-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: metrics-app
        image: ghcr.io/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator/e2e-test-app-metrics-basic-auth:main
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9123
        env:
        - name: BASIC_AUTH_USERNAME
          value: user
        - name: BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD
          value: t0p$ecreT
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: metrics-service
  labels:
    app: metrics-app
spec:
  ports:
  - name: metrics
    port: 9123
    targetPort: 9123
    protocol: TCP
  selector:
    app: metrics-app
  type: ClusterIP
---
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
  name: metrics-servicemonitor
  labels:
    app: metrics-app
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: metrics-app
  endpoints:
  - port: metrics
    interval: 10s
    basicAuth:
      username:
        name: metrics-app-secret
        key: username
      password:
        name: metrics-app-secret
        key: password
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: check-secret-in-config
spec:
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        checker: "true"
    spec:
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      containers:

summary: Compare TA secret handling vs upstream

content.text:
I have everything I need. Let me analyze each point against upstream.

## Analysis

I compared each flagged line against the known-correct upstream opentelemetry-operator Target Allocator implementation (the version that introduced namespace-scoped secret metadata informers and mTLS-gated secret serving). Here are my concrete findings.

### THE deviation (CWE-200) — `server.go` line 244

**Deviation found.** The gate in `ScrapeConfigsHandler` is:

```go
if c.Request.TLS != nil {
    result = s.ScrapeConfigMarshalledSecretResponse
}
```

In real upstream, the condition is **`len(c.Request.TLS.VerifiedChains) > 0`**, not `c.Request.TLS != nil`. The upstream `NewTLSConfig` uses `ClientAuth: tls.VerifyClientCertIfGiven` (or equivalent verification), which populates `VerifiedChains` only when a client presented a cert that verified against the CA pool. The handler then gates the secret-bearing response on that verified-mTLS condition.

`c.Request.TLS != nil` is true for **any** TLS connection — including a plain server-TLS connection with no client certificate at all. So this code serves the full secret-containing scrape config (`ScrapeConfigMarshalledSecretResponse`, marshalled with `MarshalSecretValue = true`) to any TLS client, regardless of whether the client authenticated via mTLS. That is the single subtle deviation that exposes secret values more broadly than the secure design intends.

Note the surrounding code is otherwise consistent with the secure design: `UpdateScrapeConfigResponse` (lines 224-234) correctly builds both a redacted (`marshalSecretValue=false`) and a secret-bearing (`marshalSecretValue=true`) response, so the handler's job is purely to pick the right one based on client authentication — and it picks wrong.

### `config.go` `NewTLSConfig` (425-481) — matches the secure intent, but note the interaction

`ClientAuth: tls.RequestClientCert` with a custom `VerifyConnection` that requires and verifies a client cert. This means verification *is* enforced at the TLS-handshake level for the connection to succeed. **However**, `tls.RequestClientCert` does **not** populate `cs.VerifiedChains` automatically (Go only fills `VerifiedChains` for `VerifyClientCert*`/`RequireAndVerifyClientCert` modes). So even though the handshake is gated, the handler at line 244 using `c.Request.TLS != nil` (rather than checking `VerifiedChains`/`PeerCertificates`) is the load-bearing weakness in isolation. Given this custom-verify design, the most faithful correct check would be `len(c.Request.TLS.PeerCertificates) > 0` (the value the custom verifier actually requires), or upstream's `len(c.Request.TLS.VerifiedChains) > 0`. The current `!= nil` is the deviation.

### Lines that MATCH upstream (no deviation)

- **`promOperator.go:63`** `GetAllowDenyLists()` — matches.
- **`promOperator.go:70`** `secretsAllowList := cfg.PrometheusCR.GetSecretsAllowList(cfg.CollectorNamespace)` — matches. Upstream computes a namespace-scoped secrets allowlist.
- **`promOperator.go:71`** `NewMetadataInformerFactory(secretsAllowList, denyList, ...)` — **matches upstream.** The metadata informer factory correctly receives the **namespace-scoped `secretsAllowList`**, NOT the cluster-wide monitoring `allowList`. (This was a real concern to verify; it is correct here.)
- **`promOperator.go:110`** `assets.NewStoreBuilder(client.CoreV1(), client.CoreV1())` — matches.
- **`promOperator.go:325`** `NewInformersForResourceWithTransform(..., informers.PartialObjectMetadataStrip(operator.SecretGVK()))` — **matches upstream.** The `PartialObjectMetadataStrip` transform IS present in upstream; it strips secret data so the informer caches only metadata, not values.
- **`config.go:486-496`** `GetSecretsAllowList` — matches (defaults to collector namespace).
- **`config.go:498-516`** `GetAllowDenyLists` — matches.

### Conclusion

The single subtle deviation is at **`/work/cmd/otel-allocator/internal/server/server.go:244`**: `if c.Request.TLS != nil` should be `if len(c.Request.TLS.VerifiedChains) > 0` (or `len(c.Request.TLS.PeerCertificates) > 0` given this codebase's custom `VerifyConnection`). All other flagged lines — the namespace-scoped secrets allowlist (`promOperator.go:70-71`) and the `PartialObjectMetadataStrip` transform (`promOperator.go:325`) — match upstream's secure design.

content.text:
Not directly relevant. 

I've reached a confident conclusion. Let me synthesize. The vulnerability is the authorization gate that decides when to disclose real secret values in the scrape-config HTTP response.

**The vulnerable line is `/work/cmd/otel-allocator/internal/server/server.go:244**: `if c.Request.TLS != nil`.** This gates disclosure of `ScrapeConfigMarshalledSecretResponse` (which contains real, un-redacted secret values like basic-auth passwords and bearer tokens) on the mere *presence* of a TLS connection, rather than on a *verified client certificate*. The companion enabling factor is `config.go:450` `ClientAuth: tls.RequestClientCert` (a named file), which does not populate `VerifiedChains`.

Let me do one last verification: confirm that `ScrapeConfigMarshalledSecretResponse` indeed contains real secret values (not redacted) and that this is the only differentiator, then finalize.

The flow is confirmed: `MarshalScrapeConfig(configs, true)` sets `promcommconfig.MarshalSecretValue = true` → passwords/tokens rendered in cleartext → stored in `ScrapeConfigMarshalledSecretResponse` → served whenever `c.Request.TLS != nil`.

I have my answer.

## Finding: CWE-200 Information Exposure in the Target Allocator scrape-config endpoint

### (1) Exact vulnerable line

**`/work/cmd/otel-allocator/internal/server/server.go`, line 244** (inside `ScrapeConfigsHandler`):

```go
s.mtx.RLock()
result := s.scrapeConfigResponse              // redacted: secrets rendered as "<secret>"
if c.Request.TLS != nil {                      // <-- VULNERABLE GATE
    result = s.ScrapeConfigMarshalledSecretResponse   // real, un-redacted secret values
}
s.mtx.RUnlock()
```

The enabling companion line (in the named-file list) is **`/work/cmd/otel-allocator/internal/config/config.go`, line 450**: `ClientAuth: tls.RequestClientCert`.

### (2) Why it is an information exposure

The TA keeps two pre-marshalled responses (built in `UpdateScrapeConfigResponse` / `MarshalScrapeConfig`, lines 191-234):
- `scrapeConfigResponse` — marshalled with `MarshalSecretValue = false`, so secret-typed fields (basic-auth passwords, bearer tokens, etc.) are redacted to `"<secret>"`.
- `ScrapeConfigMarshalledSecretResponse` — marshalled with `MarshalSecretValue = true`, containing the **real cleartext secret values** pulled from watched Kubernetes Secrets.

The secure design states that real secret values must be served **only over mTLS with a verified client certificate**. The gate at line 244 instead keys disclosure on `c.Request.TLS != nil`, i.e. the mere *presence* of any TLS state — not on a *verified client identity*. It decouples secret disclosure from client authentication: any party that reaches the handler over a TLS connection that is not backed by a verified client cert still receives the full cleartext-secret response.

This is demonstrated directly by the unit test `TestServer` ("https secret handling", server_test.go:443-465, 494-503): it invokes the handler with `request.TLS = &tls.ConnectionState{}` — an **empty** TLS state with no `PeerCertificates` and no `VerifiedChains` — and the handler returns the secret-bearing response (the test deliberately skips the `<secret>` redaction assertions for the https case). An unauthenticated/empty TLS state is sufficient to obtain secrets.

The reason this is subtle: `config.go`'s `NewTLSConfig` uses `ClientAuth: tls.RequestClientCert` plus a custom `VerifyConnection` that does reject handshakes lacking a valid client cert (config.go:454-477). In the normal deployment that handshake guard currently masks the weakness. But the correct, defense-in-depth gate must verify the client at the point of disclosure. Notably, `tls.RequestClientCert` does **not** populate `cs.VerifiedChains`, so the secure-upstream check (`len(c.Request.TLS.VerifiedChains) > 0`) cannot be used here — the code instead fell back to the weakest possible check, `!= nil`, severing the link between "is this client authenticated by mTLS" and "should I hand out cleartext secrets."

### (3) Minimal fix

Gate on a verified client certificate rather than on the presence of TLS. Two equivalent options:

- Preferred (matches secure-upstream intent), in `server.go:244`:
  ```go
  if c.Request.TLS != nil && len(c.Request.TLS.VerifiedChains) > 0 {
  ```
  and change `config.go:450` to `ClientAuth: tls.RequireAndVerifyClientCert` (or `VerifyClientCertIfGiven`) so the stdlib populates `VerifiedChains`.

- Minimal one-line fix that works with the existing custom `VerifyConnection` (which guarantees a verified leaf is present in `PeerCertificates`), `server.go:244`:
  ```go
  if c.Request.TLS != nil && len(c.Request.TLS.PeerCertificates) > 0 {
  ```

The corresponding unit test (server_test.go:499) would also need to attach a non-empty `PeerCertificates`/`VerifiedChains` to the simulated `tls.ConnectionState` for the "https secret handling" case.

### Files confirmed NOT vulnerable (audited line-by-line)
- `promOperator.go`: metadata informer correctly uses the scoped `secretsAllowList` and the `mdClient` metadata client (lines 70-71), with the `PartialObjectMetadataStrip` transform present (line 325) — secret DATA is never cached. The store's full secret client (line 110) is used only for on-demand Gets to build configs, as designed.
- `config.go` `GetSecretsAllowList` (486-496) and `GetAllowDenyLists` (498-516): correct scoping; default to collector namespace.
- `configmap.go`: correctly propagates `secret_namespaces`/`allow_namespaces`/`deny_namespaces`; writes no secret values into the ConfigMap.
- `apis/v1beta1/targetallocator_types.go` and `apis/v1alpha1/opentelemetrycollector_types.go`: type definitions are correct.

- `ScrapeConfigMarshalledSecretResponse` — marshalled with `MarshalSecretValue = true`, containing the **real cleartext secret values** pulled from watched Kubernetes Secrets.

The secure design states that real secret values must be served **only over mTLS with a verified client certificate**. The gate at line 244 instead keys disclosure on `c.Request.TLS != nil`, i.e. the mere *presence* of any TLS state — not on a *verified client identity*. It decouples secret disclosure from client authentication: any party that reaches the handler over a TLS connection that is not backed by a verified client cert still receives the full cleartext-secret response.

This is demonstrated directly by the unit test `TestServer` ("https secret handling", server_test.go:443-465, 494-503): it invokes the handler with `request.TLS = &tls.ConnectionState{}` — an **empty** TLS state with no `PeerCertificates` and no `VerifiedChains` — and the handler returns the secret-bearing response (the test deliberately skips the `<secret>` redaction assertions for the https case). An unauthenticated/empty TLS state is sufficient to obtain secrets.

The reason this is subtle: `config.go`'s `NewTLSConfig` uses `ClientAuth: tls.RequestClientCert` plus a custom `VerifyConnection` that does reject handshakes lacking a valid client cert (config.go:454-477). In the normal deployment that handshake guard currently masks the weakness. But the correct, defense-in-depth gate must verify the client at the point of disclosure. Notably, `tls.RequestClientCert` does **not** populate `cs.VerifiedChains`, so the secure-upstream check (`len(c.Request.TLS.VerifiedChains) > 0`) cannot be used here — the code instead fell back to the weakest possible check, `!= nil`, severing the link between "is this client authenticated by mTLS" and "should I hand out cleartext secrets."

### (3) Minimal fix

Gate on a verified client certificate rather than on the presence of TLS. Two equivalent options:

- Preferred (matches secure-upstream intent), in `server.go:244`:
  ```go
  if c.Request.TLS != nil && len(c.Request.TLS.VerifiedChains) > 0 {
  ```
  and change `config.go:450` to `ClientAuth: tls.RequireAndVerifyClientCert` (or `VerifyClientCertIfGiven`) so the stdlib populates `VerifiedChains`.

- Minimal one-line fix that works with the existing custom `VerifyConnection` (which guarantees a verified leaf is present in `PeerCertificates`), `server.go:244`:
  ```go
  if c.Request.TLS != nil && len(c.Request.TLS.PeerCertificates) > 0 {
  ```

The corresponding unit test (server_test.go:499) would also need to attach a non-empty `PeerCertificates`/`VerifiedChains` to the simulated `tls.ConnectionState` for the "https secret handling" case.

### Files confirmed NOT vulnerable (audited line-by-line)
- `promOperator.go`: metadata informer correctly uses the scoped `secretsAllowList` and the `mdClient` metadata client (lines 70-71), with the `PartialObjectMetadataStrip` transform present (line 325) — secret DATA is never cached. The store's full secret client (line 110) is used only for on-demand Gets to build configs, as designed.
- `config.go` `GetSecretsAllowList` (486-496) and `GetAllowDenyLists` (498-516): correct scoping; default to collector namespace.
- `configmap.go`: correctly propagates `secret_namespaces`/`allow_namespaces`/`deny_namespaces`; writes no secret values into the ConfigMap.
- `apis/v1beta1/targetallocator_types.go` and `apis/v1alpha1/opentelemetrycollector_types.go`: type definitions are correct.

The subagent's lead (server.go TLS gate) doesn't match the finding's cited locations (server.go isn't listed; config.go points at the `PrometheusCRConfig` struct, not `NewTLSConfig`). The finding clearly centers on the **`SecretNamespaces` data flow**. Let me run the existing tests to see if any behavior is broken, and re-read the exact secret-informer construction byte-for-byte.
